title: worker/src/mcp.ts shipped in PR #33 (D0023, 2026-05-04) is 1003 lines of handrolled JSON-RPC dispatch with zero MCP SDK dependencies — direct contradiction of the day-3 journal Constraint row
content: Verification against the merged commit (778a911 on main) confirms: worker/package.json declares only @cloudflare/workers-types, typescript, wrangler — no @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, no agents package. worker/package-lock.json (1606 lines) resolves zero MCP-related packages. worker/src/mcp.ts header line 2 states the implementation explicitly: "Implements the Streamable HTTP transport (POST = JSON-RPC request/response, GET = SSE for server-pushed notifications)." Lines 53-74 define local JsonRpcRequest/JsonRpcResponse/JsonRpcNotification interfaces. Line 76 hardcodes MCP_PROTOCOL_VERSION as "2025-06-18". Lines 118, 126-140 hand-dispatch initialize, ping, notifications/initialized, tools/list, tools/call. Lines 487, 499 hand-build JSON-RPC envelopes. Imports are exclusively local files (./auth, ./conversation, ./conversations, ./types, ./util). The day-3 journal Constraint row (2026-05-03-day3-mcp-sdk-migration.tsv, quality 4/4) explicitly named: "The hosted /mcp endpoint (SessionDO) — when it lands — uses Cloudflare `agents/mcp` McpAgent, NOT a port of this stdio server." That constraint was on disk three days before D0023 landed; the session that built mcp.ts did not search journal-side constraints and did not surface the contradiction.

title: The agent-attention failure mode is not "the rule did not exist"; it is "the rule was not surfaceable by the executing session's preflight"
content: Six MCP server projects across six months exhibited the same handroll. The corrective conversation has happened five times in chat without producing canon that binds the next session. P0002 (klappy://docs/promotions/P0002-borrow-evaluation-before-implementation) was drafted three days ago to encode the rule upstream; promotion artifact landed but the three coordinated canon edits (Bide as 6th B, the borrow-evaluation-before-implementation constraint, the bootstrap bullet) remain operator-pending per klappy.dev/AGENTS.md "Canon is read-only." The day-3 AMS journal Constraint row was on disk but journal entries are not in the oddkit canon search index by default — they are reference material, not search-surfaceable canon. SPEC.md §11 line 198 simultaneously declared "Default: hand-roll Streamable HTTP framing in mcp.ts" — a top-level repo-doc instruction that contradicted the journal Constraint row. The session building D0023 followed the higher-altitude doc (SPEC) over reference material (journal). The structural lesson: a canon rule that is not searchable in oddkit_search and not reflected in the highest-altitude repo docs (SPEC, AGENTS.md, README) will not bind a fresh agent session even when it is technically encoded somewhere on disk.

title: Created ams://canon/constraints/mcp-build-side-governance as a tier-2 AMS-local constraint binding any MCP wrapper to the maintained SDK; handroll requires a P0002-criterion justification
content: The new constraint codifies what the day-3 journal Constraint row asserted, in canon form that is searchable via oddkit and that explicitly names: required SDK per runtime (Cloudflare agents/mcp McpAgent for Workers, @modelcontextprotocol/sdk for stdio); what is forbidden (re-implementing JSON-RPC framing, handshake, transport, capabilities, notification dispatch); the justified-handroll escape hatch keyed to P0002's inspection criteria (vision conflict, foundational gap, gross overcomplication, opinionated stack imposition, improper authority, persistent gap); the day-3 stdio migration as load-bearing counter-example; tensions and reversibility (rule is itself reversible if the SDK becomes a net negative); success criteria (oddkit_search surfaces the rule before the agent writes MCP code); disconfirmer (SDK becomes consistently worse than handroll across multiple attempts). Tier-2, semi_stable, derives_from the day-3 journal + P0002 + wrapper-stays-cheap + mcp-wrapper-conformance-for-conversational-ai + the 5B method + doing-less-enables-more. Differentiates from mcp-wrapper-conformance: that constraint governs surface (what the wrapper exposes); this constraint governs substrate (how the wrapper is built). Both must hold.

title: Patched SPEC.md §11 line 198 from "Default: hand-roll" to "Resolved 2026-05-03: SDK per journal C-row + new build-side governance constraint"
content: The contradicting line in SPEC §11 is the proximate cause of D0023's handroll. Patched in-place (rather than removed and renumbered) to preserve §11's "Open Decisions Inside v1 Scope" structure, with a "Resolved 2026-05-03" prefix and explicit references to the day-3 journal, the new constraint, and P0002. The patch also documents the existing worker/src/mcp.ts as carry-over debt to be resolved by a separate scheduled rewrite, not as part of this canon PR.

title: The existing worker/src/mcp.ts (handrolled, 1003 lines) is documented debt; rewrite to agents/mcp McpAgent is a separate scheduled change
content: This canon PR does not rewrite mcp.ts. The rewrite is a code change with regression risk, deployment risk, and test surface that warrants its own planning conversation. The constraint binds new and modified MCP wrapper code from this date forward; the existing handroll is grandfathered with the explicit understanding that it will be replaced. Scheduling and scope of the rewrite is the operator's call. The build-side governance constraint surfaces the rewrite as a "What This Is Not" item explicitly so the next agent does not interpret silence as approval of the existing handroll.

title: klappy.dev/docs/promotions/P0002 has been updated with this incident as the sixth evidence row; the three canon edits P0002 proposes (Bide in the method, the borrow-evaluation-before-implementation constraint, the bootstrap bullet) remain operator-pending per AGENTS.md
content: P0002 already exists in klappy.dev as a proposed promotion artifact. Per klappy.dev/AGENTS.md, canon is read-only to agents — the three coordinated canon edits P0002 proposes (canon/methods/borrow-bend-break-beget-build, canon/constraints/borrow-evaluation-before-implementation, canon/bootstrap/model-operating-contract) require operator execution. This PR adds the AMS hosted /mcp wrapper handroll as the sixth evidence row in P0002's Evidence section to update the empirical base from N=5 to N=6, and surfaces in the operator notes that the canon edits remain pending three days after the promotion artifact landed. The unbreaking-the-loop move that depends on operator execution: apply P0002's three coordinated canon edits in klappy.dev so future Claude sessions starting on a new MCP project hit the rule via search and bootstrap, not just via this AMS-local constraint.
